purpose: Replaces narrow RV-1 (single idempotency fragment check) with a complete normative_fragment_migration_check for every numbered step of BEM-932 v8.3. For each fragment: migrated / implemented / explicitly retired / UNRESOLVED. archive_allowed=true requires zero UNRESOLVED rows.

normative_fragment_migration_check.evidence: provider_config.json v1.5 (BEM-950) inverts this: primary=claude_code, gpt_codex_cloud DISABLED_BY_COST_POLICY. This is a deliberate later policy change (BEM-950, operator decision on paid API), not a silently dropped norm.

normative_fragment_migration_check.fragment: 1.B provider_router.py JSON contract (provider_selected/fallback_reason/decision_source/trace_id/ttl_seconds/stale_ignored), TTL-scoped error check (not full results/ scan)
normative_fragment_migration_check.evidence: scripts/provider_router.py existence confirmed; whether it implements the stale-TTL-scoped check specifically (vs. naive full-folder scan) has NOT been verified by reading its current body in this protocol.

normative_fragment_migration_check.fragment: 1.C provider-router.yml routes claude_code to existing claude.yml (not a nonexistent claude-local.yml)
normative_fragment_migration_check.evidence: Confirmed in BEM-008/009 sessions: provider-router.yml exists and claude.yml is the proven live path (BEM-947).

normative_fragment_migration_check.fragment: 1.C2 three pre-flight tests (stale_quota_ignored / same_trace_quota_fallback / no_status_primary) before any live dispatch
normative_fragment_migration_check.evidence: No receipt found in this protocol session confirming these three test cases exist as code or have ever passed.

normative_fragment_migration_check.fragment: 1.D Cloudflare Worker routes via ROUTER_WORKFLOW_ID feature flag (not hardcoded), with verified rollback path
normative_fragment_migration_check.evidence: Not independently re-verified after BEM-950 provider changes. Worker code not re-read in this session.

normative_fragment_migration_check.fragment: 1.E idempotent fallback outbox notification (one trace_id -> max one Telegram message)
normative_fragment_migration_check.evidence: Confirmed earlier audit pass: idempotency check present per BEM-932 0bis.4 acceptance, referenced in original migration check that triggered GPT's BEM-009A item 7 critique.
AGENTS.md confirmed present and active in runtime_dependency_manifest.json.

normative_fragment_migration_check.fragment: 2.2-2.4 analyst/auditor/executor stage runners call provider-router.yml and use prompt_assembler instead of hardcoded prompts
normative_fragment_migration_check.evidence: prompt_assembler.py and its config (element_prompt_profiles.json, rule_registry.json) confirmed FOUND/WIRED in architecture_plan_fact_gap.json, but explicit confirmation that *_stage_runner.py CALLS it (vs. legacy hardcoded prompt) remains an open gap — already flagged honestly in plan/fact/gap row 'Prompt assembly'.

normative_fragment_migration_check.fragment: 2.5 curator sends final Telegram report per contract v3.0 format A8
normative_fragment_migration_check.evidence: Not independently verified in this protocol session.

normative_fragment_migration_check.fragment: 3.1 Worker dispatches provider-router.yml not codex-local.yml directly, getWebhookInfo <1s confirmed
normative_fragment_migration_check.evidence: Not independently re-verified after subsequent architecture changes.

normative_fragment_migration_check.fragment: 4.1 Live Telegram E2E full chain GD->DIR->WRK->WRK-Cx with real LLM
normative_fragment_migration_check.evidence: This is the SAME gap already tracked as BEM949-P3 DONE_LIMITED_SCOPE in architecture_plan_fact_gap.json. Not silently dropped — it is the same unresolved item under a different protocol number.

normative_fragment_migration_check.fragment: 4.2 RELEASE gate: WORKING_CONTOUR_READY requires external auditor APPROVED + live fallback test + BEM932-EXPERIENCE-FIX (0.3) done
normative_fragment_migration_check.evidence: This release gate logic is the direct ancestor of the current BEM-949 P7 gate and BEM-010 release_status model. Not dropped — evolved into the current release decision model.

normative_fragment_migration_check.fragment: 5.1-5.4 RULE.SYSTEM.COUNCIL_* rules, profile wiring, prompt_assembler, comparative test vs baseline
normative_fragment_migration_check.evidence: 5.1 (rules in registry) and 5.2/5.3 (profiles + assembler) confirmed present in rule_enforcement_matrix.json. 5.4 (comparative baseline test) status UNRESOLVED — no receipt found confirming this specific comparison was run.

normative_fragment_migration_check.fragment: 6.1-6.3 SYSTEM_STATUS.md update, AGENT_CONTEXT.md update, final Telegram report per contract A8
normative_fragment_migration_check.evidence: BEM-009/010 replace this with the derived-document regeneration pipeline (BEM-010 task DOC-1) rather than manual SYSTEM_STATUS.md/AGENT_CONTEXT.md edits. Intent preserved (keep status docs current), mechanism modernized.

entries.notes: 6 of 14 fragments are UNRESOLVED — meaning not verified migrated, implemented, or explicitly retired. Per the archive_allowed rule, BEM-932 v8.3 stays in active governance/protocols/ until each UNRESOLVED fragment is individually checked off by Curator-Executor in roadmap task RV-1 (BEM-010 version, expanded scope).
